bibliographic_data.title: Addressing Tactic Volatility in Self-Adaptive Systems Using Evolved Recurrent Neural Networks and Uncertainty Reduction Tactics
bibliographic_data.abstract: Self-adaptive systems frequently use tactics to perform adaptations, such as implementing additional security measures or activating cooling mechanisms. Tactic volatility occurs in real-world systems and is defined as variable behavior in the attributes of a tactic, such as its latency or cost. To enable systems' efficiency against tactic volatility, we propose a Tactic Volatility Aware (TVA-E) process utilizing evolved Recurrent Neural Networks (eRNN) to provide accurate tactic predictions. TVA-E also takes advantage of uncertainty reduction tactics to reduce uncertainty in the decision-making process. Simulations using 52,106 tactic records demonstrate that eRNN is an effective prediction mechanism and that TVA-E represents an improvement over existing state-of-the-art processes in accounting for tactic volatility.

core_content.novelty_claim: The work proposes a Tactic Volatility Aware (TVA-E) process utilizing evolved Recurrent Neural Networks (eRNN) to provide accurate tactic predictions and integrates uncertainty reduction tactics to reduce uncertainty in the decision-making process.
core_content.key_methodology: The method involves using eRNNs to predict tactic attributes, which are then incorporated into the system's existing decision-making process. Uncertainty reduction tactics are also integrated into the adaptation control loop to improve the quality and quantity of knowledge used in the decision-making process.
outcomes: eRNN is an effective prediction mechanism for tactic volatility
TVA-E represents an improvement over existing state-of-the-art processes in accounting for tactic volatility
Uncertainty reduction tactics are beneficial in accounting for tactic volatility
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper addresses the issue of tactic volatility in self-adaptive systems, where attributes such as latency and cost can vary unpredictably. To tackle this problem, the authors propose a Tactic Volatility Aware (TVA-E) process that uses evolved Recurrent Neural Networks (eRNNs) to predict tactic attributes accurately. These predictions are then integrated into the system's decision-making process, along with uncertainty reduction tactics to improve the quality and quantity of knowledge used in making decisions. Simulations using 52,106 tactic records demonstrate the effectiveness of eRNN for predicting tactic volatility and show that TVA-E outperforms existing methods in accounting for tactic volatility.
